skill.name: amazon-product-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users extract structured product listings from Amazon, including titles, ASINs, prices, ratings, and specifications. Use this skill when users want to search for products on Amazon, find the best selling brand products, track price changes for items, get a list of categories with high ratings, compare different brand products on Amazon, extract Amazon product data for market research, look for products in a specific language or marketplace, analyze competitor pricing for keywords, find featured products for search terms, get technical specifications like material or color for product lists.
skill.summary: Extract structured Amazon product data including titles, ASINs, prices, ratings, and specifications for market research and competitive analysis.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: All 63 static findings evaluated as false positives or legitimate patterns. Network access is limited to the BrowserAct API for product scraping. Environment access is restricted to the required API key. External command patterns in SKILL.md are markdown code formatting, not shell execution.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Formatting Misidentified as Shell Commands
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged markdown backticks in SKILL.md (lines 20-83) as shell backtick execution. These are actually markdown inline code formatting for variable names like `BROWSERACT_API_KEY` and `KeyWords`. No shell command execution occurs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed the actual file content and confirmed all backticks are markdown code formatting, not shell commands. No backtick-based command execution exists in the file.

low_findings.title: Documentation Text Misidentified as Cryptographic Code
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines in SKILL.md as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm'. Review confirms no cryptographic code exists. The analyzer likely misinterprets words like 'hash' appearing in documentation context.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic functions, encoding, or hashing operations found in the codebase. Pattern match triggered by documentation text containing technical terms.

low_findings.title: Logging Statements Misidentified as System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged print statements in amazon_product_api.py (lines 44-50) as system reconnaissance. These are standard logging statements that output task status and timestamps.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review confirms these are logging/print statements for task status monitoring, not reconnaissance operations.

content.user_title: Extract Amazon product data for market research
content.value_statement: Manually collecting Amazon product data is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill automates extraction of product titles, prices, ratings, and specifications from Amazon search results for efficient market research and competitive analysis.
actual_capabilities: Search Amazon for products by keywords with brand filtering
Extract product titles, ASINs, prices, ratings, and specifications
Paginate through multiple pages of search results
Support multiple language interfaces (English, Chinese, German)
Return structured JSON data ready for analysis or database import
Monitor task execution status with real-time logging
limitations: Requires a BrowserAct API key for authentication
Cannot bypass Amazon anti-scraping measures or CAPTCHAs
Rate limited by BrowserAct API quotas and task execution time
Results depend on Amazon search result structure which may change

use_cases.title: Competitive price analysis
use_cases.description: Track pricing trends for competitor products across multiple pages. Extract current and original prices to identify discount patterns and pricing strategies.

use_cases.title: Product market research
use_cases.description: Analyze top-selling products in a category by extracting ratings, review counts, and featured badges like 'Best Seller' or 'Amazon's Choice'.

use_cases.title: Product catalog enrichment
use_cases.description: Build or update product databases with structured data including ASINs, URLs, brand names, and technical specifications like material or color.

prompt_templates.title: Basic product search
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the amazon-product-api-skill to search Amazon for [keyword] products by [brand]. Extract titles, prices, and ratings.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-page market research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon for [product category] from brand [brand name]. Scan [number] pages to get a comprehensive view of available products and their average ratings.

prompt_templates.title: Price comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the top-rated [product type] under [price range] from [brand]. What is their current price and original price? Are there any active discounts?

prompt_templates.title: International market analysis
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Amazon in [language] for [product keyword] from brand [brand]. Compare the product offerings and pricing with the US marketplace.

output_examples.input: Search Amazon for wireless earbuds from brand Sony, 2 pages
output_examples.output:
Found 24 products:
- Sony WF-1000XM5: $229.99 (original $249.99), 4.5 stars (2,847 ratings), Best Seller
- Sony WF-C700N: $97.99, 4.3 stars (892 ratings), Amazon's Choice
- Sony WH-1000XM5: $348.00 (original $399.99), 4.6 stars (5,234 ratings)

output_examples.input: Find top-rated laptops under $1000 from Dell
output_examples.output:
Top results:
- Dell XPS 15: $999.99, 4.4 stars (1,203 ratings)
- Dell Inspiron 15: $649.99, 4.2 stars (856 ratings)
- Dell G15 Gaming: $899.99, 4.3 stars (2,456 ratings)

best_practices: Store your BrowserAct API key in the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill
Start with a single page to test your search parameters before expanding to multiple pages
Use brand filtering to narrow results to specific competitors or your own product line for accurate analysis
anti_patterns: Do not run the skill repeatedly without delay as this may trigger rate limiting from the BrowserAct API
Do not skip the brand parameter when you need competitor-specific data as results will include all brands
Do not assume the extracted data structure remains constant - verify field names after Amazon layout changes

faq.question: What is the BrowserAct API key and where do I get it?
faq.answer: The BrowserAct API key is a credential that authenticates your requests to the BrowserAct service. Get your key by visiting https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations and following the setup instructions.

faq.question: Can this skill bypass Amazon CAPTCHAs or anti-scraping measures?
faq.answer: No. This skill uses the BrowserAct API which handles browser automation internally. It does not bypass Amazon security measures and depends on BrowserAct infrastructure for data extraction.

faq.question: How many pages can I scrape in one task?
faq.answer: You can specify any number of pages using the Maximum_number_of_page_turns parameter. However, larger tasks take longer and may be subject to BrowserAct API rate limits.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports any Amazon marketplace language including English (en), Chinese (zh-CN), German (de), and others by specifying the language parameter.

faq.question: How is the data returned?
faq.answer: The skill returns structured data including product_title, asin, product_url, brand, price_current_amount, price_original_amount, rating_average, rating_count, featured badges, and product attributes like color or material.

faq.question: What happens if the task fails or times out?
faq.answer: If you see an 'Invalid authorization' error, check your API key. For other failures, the skill will automatically retry once. If the retry also fails, report the specific error to the user for manual resolution.
